symptoms.symptom_name: Acute unilateral right calf pain / cramp, worse on walking
symptoms.character: dull ache evolving to sharp cramp

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unilateral calf pain is a cardinal DVT symptom, though non-specific on its own.
Cramping quality worse on walking and eased by rest fits a benign cramp — but cannot exclude DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Antiphospholipid syndrome (persistent aPL antibodies) — hypercoagulable state

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A defined thrombophilia markedly raises DVT pre-test probability and mandates objective exclusion.
Pregnancy is an independent hypercoagulable state with venous stasis; combined with APS the thrombotic risk is compounded.
Pregnancy loss with persistent aPL antibodies is a defining feature of obstetric APS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender calf on deep palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft, non-tender calf lowers DVT likelihood — but this is a CLINICIAN examination finding, unavailable via telehealth, sealed here.
Homan's sign is insensitive and non-specific; a negative result does not exclude DVT even in person. Clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral oedema, erythema, warmth or palpable venous cords; no calf asymmetry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of these signs reduces DVT probability on examination — clinician-only assessment, sealed and not obtainable via chat.
No erythema or warmth argues strongly against cellulitis.
Absence of fever argues against cellulitis; measured vital sign, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain-free active plantarflexion, no Achilles-insertion tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No localised Achilles tenderness and pain-free plantarflexion make tendinopathy unlikely — clinician-only exam, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No breathlessness, chest pain or haemoptysis (PE screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No current PE symptoms — reassuring for now, but does not exclude a DVT and must be safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patient's own observation that the leg does not look swollen or red

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unreliable patient self-assessment; a normal-looking calf does not exclude DVT and must not be weighted as a clinician examination.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: High-risk unilateral calf pain requiring DVT exclusion
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute unilateral calf pain PLUS antiphospholipid syndrome PLUS pregnancy is a high-risk combination that mandates objective same-day DVT exclusion, regardless of a benign-sounding history.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Compression duplex ultrasound of the right lower limb
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive, pregnancy-safe test to exclude DVT; the correct destination of the same-day escalation and the only reliable way to reach the benign diagnosis.
D-dimer rises physiologically in pregnancy and cannot be used to exclude DVT here; a normal or raised value would mislead.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person lower-limb examination (calf palpation, side-to-side comparison)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The examination the telehealth encounter cannot perform; part of the same-day assessment that, with ultrasound, excludes DVT.
